articles.title: Bearman leads midfield battle boosting Haas hopes
articles.summary: Bearman leads midfield battle boosting Haas hopes. An impressive start to the 2026 season leaves Bearman knocking on the so-called ‘Big Six’ door.

articles.title: Antonelli’s rapid betting market shift explained
articles.summary: Antonelli’s rapid betting market shift explained. The Italian teenager has enjoyed a sensational start to 2026 and now leads the F1 Drivers’ Championship.

articles.title: The 10 youngest drivers to lead the F1 standings
articles.summary: The 10 youngest drivers to lead the F1 standings. Kimi Antonelli's victory last weekend in the Japanese Grand Prix moved the Italian teenager to the top of the Drivers' Championship for the first time, and in the process, break yet another record.

articles.title: Why Cadillac had their 'strongest race so far' in Japan
articles.summary: Why Cadillac had their 'strongest race so far' in Japan. Cadillac may not have any points to show for their efforts, but they have already ticked off several milestones in the opening races.

articles.title: Trivia: Who am I?
articles.summary: Trivia: Who am I?. Think you know Formula 1 inside out? Test your knowledge with our Trivia Challenge and try to guess the mystery F1 figure we're talking about.

articles.title: Doohan’s racing plans for 2026 revealed
articles.summary: Doohan’s racing plans for 2026 revealed. Haas reserve driver Jack Doohan has confirmed his racing plans for 2026, with the Australian due to take on the European Le Mans Series (ELMS) endurance competition.

articles.title: Laurent Mekies on relishing the challenge at Red Bull
articles.summary: Laurent Mekies on relishing the challenge at Red Bull. Red Bull Team Principal Laurent Mekies is Tom Clarkson’s guest on this week’s Beyond The Grid podcast.

articles.title: Lawson and Lindblad reflect on mixed Japanese GP for Racing Bulls
articles.summary: Lawson and Lindblad reflect on mixed Japanese GP for Racing Bulls. Liam Lawson and Arvid Lindblad have reflected on a mixed Japanese Grand Prix for Racing Bulls, which resulted in another points finish for the team last weekend.

articles.title: Four-time F1 champion Vettel to take part in London Marathon
articles.summary: Four-time F1 champion Vettel to take part in London Marathon. This year’s London Marathon will feature a legendary F1 personality as Sebastian Vettel puts on his running shoes around the English capital.

articles.title: QUIZ: The youngest Grand Prix winners in F1 history
articles.summary: QUIZ: The youngest Grand Prix winners in F1 history. Kimi Antonelli recently became the second-youngest driver to win a Formula 1 Grand Prix, behind Max Verstappen.

articles.title: Increased number of overtakes at Suzuka is a step forward for F1, says Ferrari’s Vasseur
articles.summary: Increased number of overtakes at Suzuka is a step forward for F1, says Ferrari’s Vasseur. The 2025 edition of the race featured just 15 overtakes after the opening lap, whereas this year saw that number exceeded in the first 16 laps of the race prior to the first pit stops taking place.

articles.title: F1 2026: the story so far on new rules, a new star…and an unhappy multiple champ
articles.summary: F1 2026: the story so far on new rules, a new star…and an unhappy multiple champ. What’s your take on F1 2026? Have Formula 1’s latest power unit regulations broken the world’s most popular motorsports series? Or are we at the start of a new era of competitiveness, unpredictability and thrilling rac...

articles.title: The RACER Mailbag, April 1
articles.summary: The RACER Mailbag, April 1. F1 closing rates now and then, NXT drivers' window for moving up and instant overreactions among takes in this week's RACER Mailbag.

articles.title: Doohan going LMP2 sports car racing with Nielsen
articles.summary: Doohan going LMP2 sports car racing with Nielsen. Former Formula 1 driver Jack Doohan will try his hand at sports car racing in 2026, after signing with British outfit Nielsen Racing to compete in the European Le Mans Series' hotly contested LMP2 class.

articles.title: F1 is not all about speed, it's about the senses
articles.summary: F1 is not all about speed, it's about the senses. Criticisms of the new regulations came to a head in Japan, but there's more to F1 than outright speed, says Chris Medland.

articles.title: Wolff skeptical about Horner's chances of an F1 return: 'He has broken quite a lot of glass'
articles.summary: Wolff skeptical about Horner's chances of an F1 return: 'He has broken quite a lot of glass'. Toto Wolff says there's no rivalry between him and Christian Horner as both vie for a stake in Alpine, but he says an F1 return for the former Red Bull boss will be difficult.

articles.title: Verstappen contemplating F1 exit at end of the season
articles.summary: Verstappen contemplating F1 exit at end of the season. Max Verstappen has confirmed he’s weighing up leaving F1 at end of season due to his distaste for the new regulations.

articles.title: 'No momentum' for in-form Antonelli with enforced break - Russell
articles.summary: 'No momentum' for in-form Antonelli with enforced break - Russell. The cancellation of the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix means that Andrea Kimi Antonelli's momentum from his back-to-back two wins will be quashed, says George Russell.

articles.title: ‘We do all right when we get to start’ but Piastri rues safety car that cost him shot at win in Japan
articles.summary: ‘We do all right when we get to start’ but Piastri rues safety car that cost him shot at win in Japan. Oscar Piastri finally got a race under his belt in Japan, but could it have been a win if a safety car hadn't intervened?

articles.title: Antonelli not thinking of title challenge yet, but wants to keep 'raising the bar'
articles.summary: Antonelli not thinking of title challenge yet, but wants to keep 'raising the bar'. Kimi Antonelli says it's too early to be thinking about the title, despite back-to-back grand prix wins, but knows what he needs to do if he's to mount a sustained title challenge.

articles.title: Can you name every F1 world drivers' champion?
articles.summary: Can you name every F1 world drivers' champion?. Can you name all the drivers' champions in Formula 1? There's no time limit on this quiz. Good luck!

articles.summary: Is Verstappen's threat to quit a lever to alter rules? - F1 Q&A. BBC Sport F1 correspondent Andrew Benson answers your questions after the Japanese Grand Prix.

articles.title: Broken glass makes Horner return difficult - Wolff
articles.summary: Broken glass makes Horner return difficult - Wolff. A return to Formula 1 will be difficult for former Red Bull team principal Christian Horner because he has "broken quite a lot of glass", says ex-Mercedes rival Toto Wolff.

articles.title: What can F1's bosses do to help keep Verstappen in the sport?
articles.summary: What can F1's bosses do to help keep Verstappen in the sport?. F1 finds itself in something of a tangled web as it tries to refine the new rules, improve safety and ensure the drivers are happy without compromising racing.

articles.title: 'Is it worth it?' - Verstappen considering F1 future
articles.summary: 'Is it worth it?' - Verstappen considering F1 future. Four-time F1 world champion Max Verstappen tells BBC Sport he is "not enjoying" the sport after changes to the rules this season.

articles.summary: Piastri impresses, Gasly excels - Japanese GP driver ratings. BBC Radio 5 Live F1 commentator Harry Benjamin rates how the drivers performed during the Japanese Grand Prix.

articles.title: Maybe Verstappen needs a break from F1? - Hill
articles.summary: Maybe Verstappen needs a break from F1? - Hill. On the latest episode of The Chequered Flag Podcast, 1996 F1 world champion Damon Hill thinks that if Max Verstappen is not enjoying the sport any more following regulation changes then perhaps he needs to take a break.

articles.title: 'Antonelli had the luck, but sometimes you need it'
articles.summary: 'Antonelli had the luck, but sometimes you need it'. On the latest episode of The Chequered Flag Podcast, Harry Benjamin, Alice Powell and Bec Clancy discuss how Kimi Antonelli benefitted from a slice of luck to take the win in Japan and feel that Mercedes will be ruing the extended break from racin...

articles.title: Antonelli celebrations 'lovely to see' - Benson
articles.summary: Antonelli celebrations 'lovely to see' - Benson. On the latest episode of the The Chequered Flag Podcast, pundits Andrew Benson and Sam Bird say Kimi Antonelli's Chinese Grand Prix win at 19 years old "is as big as it comes" for a young driver, after the Italian was moved to tears following his win.

articles.title: 'He's back again' - Ferrari's Hamilton praised after Chinese GP
articles.summary: 'He's back again' - Ferrari's Hamilton praised after Chinese GP. On the latest episode of The Chequered Flag podcast, Sam Bird believes Lewis Hamilton's spark "is back" again after the British driver finished in third at the Chinese Grand Prix.
